name: Medina County
description_short: Medina County Federal Credit Union is a community-focused credit union established in 1960, serving over 9,700 members in Wadsworth, Ohio with $123.3M in assets.
description_long:
Medina County Federal Credit Union was established in 1960 and is headquartered in Wadsworth, Ohio. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative designed to serve the local community. The institution has grown to serve over 9,700 loyal members while maintaining stewardship of $123.3 million in assets, supported by a team of over 24 dedicated employees as of 2026.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, and business loans. Members can access modern banking conveniences such as online banking, mobile app functionality, bill pay, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and electronic signature authentication. The institution also provides retirement accounts (IRA/KEOGH), financial calculators, interest rate comparison tools, and student scholarships to support community members.

Medina County FCU distinguishes itself through its deep local roots and personalized member service approach. Operating with just one branch and three ATMs, the credit union emphasizes relationship-based banking where staff reportedly know their members personally. The institution has maintained strong member loyalty, with documented members banking there since 1992, and recent reviews highlight exceptional customer service and careful financial stewardship. Their mobile and online platforms remain operational and accessible, demonstrating commitment to modern banking accessibility despite their community-focused footprint.

The primary limitation of Medina County FCU is its geographic scope—with only one branch location and three ATMs, members in surrounding areas may face convenience challenges. The credit union does not appear to offer mortgage lending services based on available information, which may limit its utility for homebuyers. Additionally, as a smaller institution, it may have fewer competitive advantages in specialized financial products compared to larger regional or national credit unions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Financial calculators and interest rate comparison tools
pros: Established in 1960 with 60+ years of operational history and community trust
Serves over 9,700 members with $123.3M in assets, providing financial stability
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, business loans, and CDs
Modern digital banking: online banking, mobile app, bill pay, mobile payments, and remote deposit capture
Perfect 5.0-star member rating based on documented reviews praising staff service and financial stewardship
Offers member benefits including student scholarships and financial calculators
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns incentives with customer interests rather than shareholder profit
cons: Limited physical footprint with only 1 branch and 3 ATMs, restricting convenience for non-local members
No mortgage lending services offered, limiting full-service banking capabilities for homebuyers
Geographic membership eligibility restrictions limit who can join to specific community boundaries
Smaller institution may offer fewer competitive rates or specialized financial products vs. larger credit unions
Current website indicates lobby and drive-thru hours are 'currently closed,' raising questions about operational status
best_for: Local Medina County, Ohio residents and businesses seeking community-focused banking with personal service
Members prioritizing member-owned cooperative structure and local accountability over national bank convenience
Savers and borrowers comfortable with smaller institution offerings who value relationship banking
diagnosis: Medina County Federal Credit Union is best for established residents of Medina County, Ohio who value personalized, relationship-based banking and community accountability over branch convenience. The main caveat is its single-location footprint and lack of mortgage services, which limits its utility for members outside the immediate Wadsworth area or those seeking comprehensive home lending products.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
